title: Isolate and quantify the dopamine and metabolites levels in the brain by HPLC
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"37po3","text":"This protocol aims to determine the levels of dopamine (DA) and its metabolites 3,4-dihydroxyphenylacetaldehyde (DOPAL) and 3,4-dihydroxyphenylacetic acid (DOPAC) in brain tissue samples. The measurement was performed by a high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) with electrochemical detection (ECD). The levels of DA, DOPAL, and DOPAC were quantified using a 5-point calibration curve prepared in 0.3 M perchloric acid and were expressed as pmol of analyte per mg of protein. Protein content in the sample pellet was determined using a bicinchoninic acid (BCA) protein assay kit following the manufacturer's instructions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ecoe","text":"Perform every extraction step on ice until samples are ready to be analyzed by HPLC-ECD.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Dissect the region of interest (e.g., striatum) and weigh the tissue (~20 mg) in a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube
Homogenize the tissues in ice-cold 0.3 M perchloric acid (PCA, 1:5 w/v) using disposable pellet pestles, rinse the pellet pestles with PCA before the next sample
Spike the internal standard (3,4-di-OH-benzylamine; 10 µL of a 20 µM working solution in PCA) to each tube
Refrigerate for 5 min, or store in -80 o C before use
Centrifuge at 10,000 g for 10 min to precipitate the protein, transfer the supernatant to a new microcentrifuge tube
Transfer 100 µL of the supernatant into a LC vial with insert. The samples are ready to run on HPLC-ECD

Purge/flush the system with mobile phase (50 mM citric acid, 1.8 mM sodium heptane sulfonate, 0.2% trifluoroacetic acid, 2% acetonitrile, pH =3.0) at 2 mL/min for a few minutes to purge the bubbles out of the system. Check if the column is leaking.
Prepare a calibration curve for DA, DOPAC, and DOPAL in 0.3 M PCA (10, 5, 2, 1, 0.5, and 0 µM)

For electrochemical detection of catechol-containing compounds, the guard cell, electrode 1, and electrode 2 were set to +350, -150, and +200 mV, respectively. Let the ECD stabilize for 30 min until the baseline is flat, then rezero the ECD.

Inject 1 µL samples (including sample extracts and calibration standards) into the HPLC. Separation isachieved with a Phenomenex Synergi C18 column (2.0 x 150 mm, 80 A) at 200 µL/min, with an elution order DOPAL (8.5 min), internal standard (11.6 min), DOPAC (14.2 min), and DA (18.6 min).
The amount of DA, DOPAC, and DOPAL was determined by comparison to the calibration curve
Vortex and resuspend the tissue pellet, neutralize the pellet with NaOH to make sure it is pH 6.5~8 using pH paper
Add 25 µL BSA standards (0-2000 µg/L) or sample supernatant in duplicate into a 96-well plate
Add 200 µL working solution into each well (working solution A:B = 50:1)
Shake the plate for 1 min
Incubate the plate with lid at 37 o C for 30 min
Read the absorption at 562 nm using a plate reader and subtract the blank value (PCA alone)
Centrifuge at 10,000 g for 10 min and take the supernatant
Calculate the protein concentration based on the calibration curve by BSA standards and report the DA, DOPAC, and DOPAL concentrations normalized for protein (pmol/mg protein)
Prepare bovine serum albumin (BSA) standard: 2000, 1500, 1000, 750, 500, 250, 125, 25, and 0 µg/L

The DA, DOPAC, and DOPAL calibration standard should be aliquoted and stored at -20 C, avoid execisive freeze-thraw cycles. Samples extracts should be archived and stored in -80 C for long-term storage. Dispose the waste properly, for example, the used tubes and tissue samples should be disposed in a biohazard bin, liquid waste should be stored in an approved, closed container compatible with the liquid's contents, in a dedicated satellite accumulation area.
creator: Hans-Joachim Lehmler
